jokes.text: Oh, absolutely brilliant! I truly believed you were Hemingway. Bravo, Martin.
jokes.setup: Martin gives a dramatic book report about Hemingway
jokes.punchline: Teacher's over-the-top praise for what's clearly just a student book report
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing teacher's overenthusiasm contrasted with reality

jokes.text: -Call me Papa. -Some ketchup for your buns, Papa?
jokes.setup: Martin asks to be called 'Papa' like Hemingway
jokes.punchline: Student immediately deflates the pretension with cafeteria talk
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of pretension with perfect economy and clever wordplay on 'Papa'

jokes.text: Students, prepare to be dazzled. The name of the book that I read was Treasure Island.
jokes.setup: Bart dramatically promises to dazzle with his book report
jokes.punchline: Immediately delivers the most basic, obvious opening possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart character moment - big promises followed by underwhelming delivery

jokes.text: And green birds on their shoulders.
jokes.setup: Bart describing pirates with patches and gold teeth
jokes.punchline: Adds the most cliched pirate detail possible - parrots
jokes.explanation: Solid character beat showing Bart's reliance on stereotypes

jokes.text: And published by McGraw-Hill. So in conclusion, on a scale of 1 to 10... 10 being the highest, 1 the lowest, and 5 average, I give it... a 9.
jokes.setup: Bart tries to sound academic by mentioning publisher and rating system
jokes.punchline: Overly complex setup for simple rating, plus giving classic a 9 (not 10) shows false sophistication
jokes.explanation: Good escalating pretension and the 9/10 detail shows Bart's false sophistication

jokes.text: Questions? No? Then I'll sit down. Did you read the book? I'm insulted. Is this a book report or a witch hunt?
jokes.setup: Teacher asks simple question about whether Bart read the book
jokes.punchline: Bart deflects with mock indignation and 'witch hunt' accusation
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment with perfect escalation and quotable 'witch hunt' line

jokes.text: Blackbeard, Captain Nemo, Captain Hook, Bluebeard.
jokes.setup: Teacher asks for the name of the pirate from Treasure Island
jokes.punchline: Bart lists every famous fictional pirate except the right one
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Bart knows pirates but didn't read the book

jokes.text: -Blah, blah, blah, blah. -Yes, ma'am. -Blah, blah, blah. -Yes, ma'am. -You haven't been paying attention! -Yes, ma'am.
jokes.setup: Teacher lecturing Bart after class
jokes.punchline: Bart's automatic 'Yes, ma'am' responses prove he's not listening even when caught
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating structure that perfectly demonstrates the problem

jokes.text: Then what did I say? -Straighten up and fly right? -Lucky guess.
jokes.setup: Teacher catches Bart not listening and demands he repeat what she said
jokes.punchline: Bart's generic advice guess is so close that teacher calls it a 'lucky guess'
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline - implies teacher's lecture was generic enough that random advice works

jokes.text: Take that, granny! Hide! Deadly mothballs. Granny's kissing me!
jokes.setup: Bart playing video game instead of studying
jokes.punchline: Video game involves fighting grandmothers with mothballs and unwanted kisses
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd video game concept that's both original and quotable

jokes.text: You reached the level of ungrateful grandchild. Try again, if you dare!
jokes.setup: Continuation of grandmother-fighting video game
jokes.punchline: Game insults player by calling them 'ungrateful grandchild' instead of typical 'game over'
jokes.explanation: Incredibly creative subversion of video game tropes with perfect family guilt angle

jokes.text: -He doesn't care, Mom. -I do. I want a beer while I'm caring.
jokes.setup: Lisa says Homer doesn't care about her news
jokes.punchline: Homer proves he cares by wanting a beer to enhance his caring experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer line - simultaneously caring and selfish with brilliant economy

jokes.text: You did! Well, that's just.... Oh, what a glorious day!
jokes.setup: Lisa announcing she got an A on her vocabulary test
jokes.punchline: Homer's over-the-top reaction to mundane school achievement
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer overreaction humor - enthusiastic but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Hand me your paper. I'll put it on the refrigerator. I might as well kill two birds with one stone.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to put Lisa's A+ paper on the refrigerator
jokes.punchline: Visual gag of Homer covering up Bart's F paper with Lisa's A
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy showing family dynamics and Homer's practical solutions

jokes.text: You covered up my paper! Look at those funny little whiskers. That reminds me.
jokes.setup: Bart protests his paper being covered
jokes.punchline: Homer gets distracted by something on TV instead of addressing Bart's complaint
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Homer's distractibility and Bart's neglect

jokes.text: It's Gorilla Week on Million Dollar Movie. No, Dad, I should really-- Gorilla the Conqueror. The granddaddy of them all. Well, maybe just one more hour.
jokes.setup: Bart tries to resist distraction to study
jokes.punchline: Homer's enthusiastic description immediately breaks Bart's resolve
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing how easily Bart is distracted and Homer enables it

jokes.text: The little tiger tries so hard. Why does he keep failing? Just a little dim, I guess.
jokes.setup: Parents watching Bart struggle to study
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual dismissal of Bart as 'dim' while showing no understanding of irony
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor showing Homer's lack of parental insight

jokes.text: Hey, Bart-dude. You look freaked. I got a test I'm not ready for. Can you crash the bus?
jokes.setup: Bart tells Otto he has a test he's not ready for
jokes.punchline: Bart's solution is to ask bus driver to crash the bus
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic - solving problems through chaos, delivered with great economy

jokes.text: Sorry, I can't do it on purpose. But maybe you'll get lucky.
jokes.setup: Bart asks Otto to crash the bus
jokes.punchline: Otto implies crashes happen regularly, just not on purpose
jokes.explanation: Excellent Otto character moment with perfect dark implication about his driving

jokes.text: -Name the pilgrim's boat. -The Spirit of St. Louis. -Where'd they land? -Sunny Acapulco. -Why'd they leave England? -Giant rats.
jokes.setup: Girls giving Bart deliberately wrong answers for his test
jokes.punchline: Each answer gets more absurd, ending with 'giant rats'
jokes.explanation: Great escalating structure with perfect absurd escalation to 'giant rats'

jokes.text: Cool, history's coming alive.
jokes.setup: Bart receiving completely wrong historical information
jokes.punchline: Bart's enthusiasm for obviously false facts
jokes.explanation: Good irony showing Bart's gullibility and misplaced enthusiasm

jokes.text: A blindfolded chimp with a pencil in his teeth has more chances than you.
jokes.setup: Martin correcting the wrong information Bart received
jokes.punchline: Elaborately specific insult about Bart's test chances
jokes.explanation: Excellent Martin insult with perfect specificity and vivid imagery

jokes.text: -What's the matter, son? -Stabbing pains in my stomach.
jokes.setup: Test is about to begin
jokes.punchline: Bart suddenly develops mysterious illness to avoid test
jokes.explanation: Predictable setup but solid character consistency for Bart's avoidance tactics

jokes.text: -Do you feel pain in your arm? -Both arms. -Temporary loss of vision? -Who said that? Come closer.
jokes.setup: Teacher checking Bart's symptoms
jokes.punchline: Bart escalates each symptom, then acts blind
jokes.explanation: Good escalating structure with perfect physical comedy timing

jokes.text: I wish I had amoria phlebitis.
jokes.setup: Lisa watching Bart get pampered for fake illness
jokes.punchline: Lisa makes up medical-sounding disease name
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa character moment with clever fake medical terminology

jokes.text: What'd you get for number one? Number two? Yeah, that sounds right.
jokes.setup: Bart asking Milhouse about test answers
jokes.punchline: Bart agrees with answer he never heard because Milhouse didn't respond
jokes.explanation: Good joke showing Bart's desperation and poor listening skills

jokes.text: This test is worse than Milhouse's!
jokes.setup: Teacher grading Bart's test
jokes.punchline: Bart's test is worse than notoriously bad student Milhouse
jokes.explanation: Good comparison joke establishing Milhouse as benchmark for failure

jokes.text: Hey, Dr. J.
jokes.setup: Formal introduction to district psychiatrist Dr. J. Loren Pryor
jokes.punchline: Bart casually greets him like a basketball player
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character consistency - Bart treating serious situation casually

jokes.text: Look at these results: 55, 42, 26. A 12 on state capitals! Why are we dancing around the obvious? We all know it. I'm dumb as a post.
jokes.setup: Adults discussing Bart's failing grades
jokes.punchline: Bart cuts through euphemisms with brutal self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment with perfect escalation and surprising self-awareness

jokes.text: Look in my eyes. See the sincerity? See the fear? As God is my witness, I can pass the fourth grade!
jokes.setup: Bart pleading not to be held back
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic Scarlett O'Hara reference for basic academic goal
jokes.explanation: Great pop culture reference applied to mundane situation with perfect drama

jokes.text: If not, at least you'll be bigger than the other kids.
jokes.setup: Bart's dramatic plea about passing fourth grade
jokes.punchline: Psychiatrist offers consolation prize of physical intimidation advantage
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of inappropriate consolation from authority figure

jokes.text: Otto, I respect you. You let us throw stuff at cars and try to tip the bus. Damn thing never goes over, does it?
jokes.setup: Bart praising Otto for being permissive
jokes.punchline: Otto's casual disappointment that bus won't tip over
jokes.explanation: Perfect Otto character moment with casual attitude toward bus safety

jokes.text: I got held back in the fourth grade. Twice. And look at me, man. Now I drive the school bus!
jokes.setup: Otto trying to console Bart about being held back
jokes.punchline: Otto's 'success story' is driving a school bus after being held back twice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - Otto's proud of a job that requires minimal education after failing repeatedly

jokes.text: I got a peptic ulcer, my wife wants a new car and I need a root canal. Quit bugging me about the pirate.
jokes.setup: Flash-forward dream of adult Bart still in fourth grade
jokes.punchline: Adult problems while still being asked elementary questions
jokes.explanation: Excellent nightmare scenario combining adult responsibilities with childhood failure

jokes.text: -Long John Silver, Dad. -I heard that, Bart Jr. I want to see both of you after class.
jokes.setup: Adult Bart's son helps him with pirate answer
jokes.punchline: Both father and son get in trouble for cheating in elementary school
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of nightmare - generational academic failure cycle

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Bart. I don't know the rules of your sport. I didn't want to interfere with a ball in play.
jokes.setup: Bart asks Martin to throw him the ball
jokes.punchline: Martin overthinks simple request with overly formal sports terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin character moment - academic overthinking of simple social interaction

jokes.text: They laugh at me? I'd always considered myself rather popular.
jokes.setup: Bart tells Martin other kids laugh at him
jokes.punchline: Martin's complete lack of self-awareness about his social status
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Martin's social obliviousness

jokes.text: My speed with numbers, my years as a hall monitor... my prize-winning dioramas, they mean nothing to them?
jokes.setup: Martin learning he's not popular
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly nerdy achievements he thought would impress peers
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating list of nerdy accomplishments showing Martin's mindset

jokes.text: No, this won't do at all. We have to clean up this room. And we'll need a fern. No room is complete without plant life.
jokes.setup: Martin seeing Bart's messy room
jokes.punchline: Immediately starts interior decorating with specific plant requirements
jokes.explanation: Good Martin character moment with specific fern detail showing his precision

jokes.text: Mischief varies inversely with proximity to the authority figure.
jokes.setup: Bart explains why geeks should sit in back
jokes.punchline: Martin translates simple concept into mathematical formula
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Martin line - perfect character voice translating street wisdom to academic speak

jokes.text: Mischief varies inversely with proximity to the authority figure.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining why geeks sit in front
jokes.punchline: Martin's academic formulation of playground wisdom
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Martin line - taking basic playground strategy and expressing it as a mathematical principle

jokes.text: Yeah, but don't say it like that.
jokes.setup: Martin's overly academic explanation of mischief
jokes.punchline: Bart's exasperated reaction to Martin's vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat showing character dynamic and Bart's social awareness

jokes.text: Pushing a boy into the girls' lavatory was such a thrill! The screams, the humiliation, the fact that it wasn't me!
jokes.setup: Martin experiencing being bad for first time
jokes.punchline: Martin gets excited about others' humiliation with academic precision
jokes.explanation: Excellent character development showing Martin's analytical approach to mischief

jokes.text: The old Martin no longer exists. Come on, fellows, to the arcade!
jokes.setup: Martin abandoning studies to help Bart
jokes.punchline: Martin dramatically declares transformation and uses word 'fellows'
jokes.explanation: Good character arc moment with Martin still using formal vocabulary while rebelling

jokes.text: Prayer, the last refuge of a scoundrel.
jokes.setup: Bart about to pray for help with test
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on his own desperation
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment showing Bart's occasional wisdom and economy of language

jokes.text: Thanking you in advance, your pal, Bart Simpson.
jokes.setup: Bart praying desperately for divine intervention
jokes.punchline: Signs off prayer like casual letter to friend
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart voice - treating sacred with casual familiarity

jokes.text: -He's Bill. -He's Marty. We can't get enough of each other.
jokes.setup: Radio DJs introducing themselves
jokes.punchline: Creepy overintimacy between morning radio hosts
jokes.explanation: Good parody of morning radio dynamic with unsettling intimacy

jokes.text: Whoa! Good morning, world!
jokes.setup: Bart waking up to see it snowed overnight
jokes.punchline: His dramatic joy at what appears to be answered prayers
jokes.explanation: Simple joy reaction - sets up the miracle payoff but not particularly clever on its own

jokes.text: I don't know who or what God is exactly. But he's more powerful than Mom and Dad put together. And you owe him big.
jokes.setup: Bart realizes his prayer was answered with snow day
jokes.punchline: Bart's practical theology based on power hierarchy and debt
jokes.explanation: Brilliant child logic about divinity with perfect character voice and quotable wisdom

jokes.text: I hereby declare this day to be Snow Day... the funnest day in the history of Springfield!
jokes.setup: Mr. Burns participating in snowball fight
jokes.punchline: Evil capitalist declares day of fun with childish grammar
jokes.explanation: Good character subversion with Burns showing childlike joy and poor grammar

jokes.text: Look, everybody! It's snowing! -In the middle of July? -It's a miracle. I've invented something fun. The sled.
jokes.setup: Bart daydreaming while studying about Continental Congress
jokes.punchline: Historical figures get distracted by impossible snow and claim to invent sleds
jokes.explanation: Creative fantasy sequence mixing historical setting with modern distractions

jokes.text: -In the middle of July? -It's a miracle. I've invented something fun. The sled.
jokes.setup: Bart's fantasy of the founding fathers in snow
jokes.punchline: Ben Franklin's anachronistic invention of the sled
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist history mixing - Ben Franklin 'inventing' the sled fits his inventor persona

jokes.text: Look, John Hancock's writing his name in the snow!
jokes.setup: Revolutionary War figures playing in snow
jokes.punchline: John Hancock, famous for large signature, writes name in snow
jokes.explanation: Brilliant historical reference joke connecting Hancock's famous signature to snow writing

jokes.text: I know. Another year together. It's gonna be hell.
jokes.setup: Bart fails test and will be held back
jokes.punchline: Teacher dreads having Bart another year more than he does
jokes.explanation: Good subversion - teacher more upset than student about grade retention

jokes.text: Now I know how Washington felt when he surrendered Fort Necessity in 1754. You know, 1754. The famous defeat to the French.
jokes.setup: Bart feels defeated after failing despite trying
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates actual historical knowledge while claiming to be stupid
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Bart shows intelligence while despairing about being dumb

jokes.text: -My God, Bart, you're right! -So? You demonstrated applied knowledge. Such an obscure reference deserves an extra point. It's only fair.
jokes.setup: Teacher realizes Bart's historical reference is correct
jokes.punchline: One point for obscure knowledge is enough to change F to D-minus
jokes.explanation: Great payoff showing knowledge coming through when least expected

jokes.text: I passed! I got a D minus! I passed! I got a D minus! I passed! l-- Kissed the teacher?!
jokes.setup: Bart celebrating passing with D-minus grade
jokes.punchline: Celebration culminates in accidentally kissing teacher, horrifying classmates
jokes.explanation: Good escalating celebration ending in perfect physical comedy disaster

jokes.text: Thanks, Dad, but part of this D minus belongs to God.
jokes.setup: Homer proud of Bart's D-minus grade
jokes.punchline: Bart credits divine intervention for his marginal success
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to prayer storyline with Bart's characteristic mix of wisdom and irreverence
